project.name: Pokemon TCG Agent Pilots
project.description: A lineage of Python AI "pilot" strategies that play Pokemon TCG decks inside an external simulator engine (cg). Each file is a self-contained agent(obs) decision function for a specific deck archetype; files relate to each other as iterations and variants of evolving strategies.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker that makes the agents directory importable as a Python package.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Ignore-list file used by the Understand-Anything tool to exclude paths from analysis.

nodes.name: bare_agent.py
nodes.summary: Baseline generic Pokemon TCG pilot with no archetype-specific logic; scores attacks, energy attachment, and bench decisions purely from the engine observation.
nodes.languageNotes: Self-contained agent built around a single large agent(obs_dict) decision function plus shared card/attack helper functions.

nodes.name: bare_agent2.py
nodes.summary: Near-identical variant of bare_agent.py providing a second baseline generic pilot for A/B comparison in the gauntlet.

nodes.name: fire.py
nodes.summary: Anti-Crustle Fire-type aggro pilot that exploits the opponent's Grass weakness; adds a heal-awareness helper on top of the generic scoring engine.
nodes.languageNotes: Extends the baseline scaffold with a _heals(cid) helper to value healing trainers/abilities.

nodes.name: honchkrow.py
nodes.summary: Combo-aware Rocket's Honchkrow pilot whose Rocket Feathers attack damage scales with discarded ammo; the strongest hand-tuned anti-meta agent in the project.
nodes.languageNotes: Adds card/attack name lookups (_card_named, _attack_named), weakness application, and a dedicated rocket_feathers_damage model on top of the shared scaffold.

nodes.name: main_bench.py
nodes.summary: Bench-focused variant of the main generic pilot that emphasizes bench-development heuristics in its decision scoring.

nodes.name: main_crustle_base.py
nodes.summary: Baseline pilot for the Crustle archetype deck, identical scaffold to main.py used as the Crustle-family reference point.

nodes.name: main_cur.py
nodes.summary: Current working main pilot variant, a snapshot of main.py used as the active comparison baseline.

nodes.name: main_honch_base.py
nodes.summary: Baseline (untuned) Honchkrow pilot using the generic scaffold, the starting point that honchkrow.py later improves with combo-aware damage.

nodes.name: main_megalucario_backup.py
nodes.summary: Backup of the original Mega Lucario pilot; the earliest deck strategy that later variants were benchmarked against and beat.
nodes.languageNotes: Largest single agent() body (~415 lines) with fewer extracted helpers, reflecting an earlier monolithic implementation style.

nodes.name: main_typh_base.py
nodes.summary: Baseline pilot for the Typhlosion archetype, the reference variant for the Crustle/Typhlosion two-line deck.

nodes.name: main_typhlosion.py
nodes.summary: Typhlosion archetype pilot (the main_v2 = Typhlosion deck); generic engine playing the Crustle/Typhlosion two-line deck.

nodes.name: main_v1.py
nodes.summary: First iteration of the evolving main pilot lineage; generic scoring engine that later versions (v2, v3, v4) build upon.

nodes.name: main_v2.py
nodes.summary: Second iteration pilot (the Typhlosion-deck generic engine) that proved strongest as an untuned generic pilot in the gauntlet.
nodes.languageNotes: Introduces _stage and _best_dmg helpers refining the v1 scaffold.

nodes.name: main_v2_baseline.py
nodes.summary: Frozen baseline copy of main_v2 used as the control for measuring whether later tuning overlays improve or regress the gauntlet.

nodes.name: main_v2_rebel.py
nodes.summary: Experimental v2 overlay adding cheap-damage, energy-acceleration, and heal heuristics; an archetype-tuning branch off main_v2.
nodes.languageNotes: Adds _heals, _cheap_dmg, and _accels heuristic helpers exploring archetype overlays.

nodes.name: main_v3.py
nodes.summary: Third iteration pilot with full combo modeling: detects Ethan's Pokemon in discard and models Buddy Blast damage scaling plus hand/field awareness.
nodes.languageNotes: Largest helper set: _ethan_in_discard, _buddy_blast_damage, _is_buddy_blast, _hand_has, _field_has feed an ethan_fuel-aware attack_damage.

nodes.name: main_v3_pure.py
nodes.summary: Strongest pilot: the v2 generic engine plus only the pure Buddy Blast real-damage correction, deliberately omitting all regressing archetype overlays.
nodes.languageNotes: Pares main_v3 back to the v2 scaffold (no combo helper sprawl), isolating the single damage-correction fact the engine cannot read.

nodes.name: main_v4.py
nodes.summary: Fourth iteration pilot retaining the Ethan's-in-discard damage correction with a trimmed helper set compared to v3.
nodes.languageNotes: Keeps _ethan_in_discard and an ethan_fuel-aware attack_damage but drops the broader buddy-blast/hand/field helpers of v3.

nodes.name: main.py
nodes.summary: Primary entry-point pilot for the project, currently running the Crustle-family generic decision engine that the other variants are benchmarked against.

nodes.name: agent
nodes.summary: Main decision entry point: parses the observation, enumerates legal moves, and selects an action by scoring attacks, energy attachment, evolution, and bench plays.

nodes.name: agent
nodes.summary: Decision entry point for the second baseline pilot, scoring and selecting moves from the engine observation.

nodes.name: agent
nodes.summary: Fire-deck decision entry point that biases scoring toward exploiting Grass-weak opponents and values healing effects.

nodes.name: agent
nodes.summary: Honchkrow decision entry point that plans Rocket Feathers ammo discards and selects attacks using the combo-aware damage model.

nodes.name: rocket_feathers_damage
nodes.summary: Computes Rocket Feathers attack damage scaled by available ammo and weakness, the core combo-correction the engine cannot infer.

nodes.name: agent
nodes.summary: Bench-oriented decision entry point that scores moves with added weight on bench development.

nodes.name: agent
nodes.summary: Crustle-baseline decision entry point using the shared generic scoring engine.

nodes.name: agent
nodes.summary: Current-main decision entry point, an active snapshot of the generic scoring engine.

nodes.name: agent
nodes.summary: Untuned Honchkrow-baseline decision entry point using the generic engine without combo damage modeling.

nodes.name: agent
nodes.summary: Monolithic Mega Lucario decision entry point, the project's earliest full-game move-selection logic.

nodes.name: agent
nodes.summary: Typhlosion-baseline decision entry point using the shared generic scoring engine.

nodes.name: agent
nodes.summary: Typhlosion-deck decision entry point driving the Crustle/Typhlosion two-line strategy.

nodes.name: agent
nodes.summary: First-iteration decision entry point that establishes the generic scoring engine reused by later versions.

nodes.name: agent
nodes.summary: Second-iteration decision entry point; the strongest untuned generic pilot in the gauntlet.

nodes.name: agent
nodes.summary: Frozen v2-baseline decision entry point used as the control for tuning experiments.

nodes.name: agent
nodes.summary: Experimental v2 decision entry point layering cheap-damage, energy-acceleration, and heal heuristics over the baseline scoring.

nodes.name: agent
nodes.summary: Third-iteration decision entry point integrating Ethan's-in-discard detection and Buddy Blast damage scaling into move scoring.

nodes.name: _buddy_blast_damage
nodes.summary: Models the real Buddy Blast attack damage (40 + 60 per Ethan's Pokemon in discard) that the simulator engine cannot read.

nodes.name: agent
nodes.summary: Strongest pilot's decision entry point: the v2 engine with only the isolated Buddy Blast damage correction applied.

nodes.name: agent
nodes.summary: Fourth-iteration decision entry point retaining the Ethan's-in-discard damage correction with a trimmed helper set.

nodes.name: agent
nodes.summary: Primary entry-point decision function that selects the project's default deck moves via the generic scoring engine.

layers.name: Generic Engine & Baselines
layers.description: Archetype-agnostic auto-role pilots that form the lineage backbone, including the main.py decision engine (fan-in 4) and its v1/v2 iterations plus bare and bench baselines that every other pilot descends from.

layers.name: Crustle / Typhlosion Line
layers.description: Dual-line Crustle/Typhlosion deck pilots derived from main and main_v2, representing the strongest gauntlet engine in the lineage.

layers.name: Buddy Blast Damage-Correction Series (v3-v4)
layers.description: The ablation series branching off main_v2 that layers in the Buddy Blast real-damage correction, including the v3 base, v4 extension, and the mirror-neutral v3_pure isolate.

layers.name: Honchkrow Combo
layers.description: Combo-aware Honchkrow pilots with precise ammo-discard logic; main_honch_base feeds back into the v1 lineage via the related chain.

layers.name: Fire Counter Deck
layers.description: Anti-Crustle Fire aggro counter pilots that combine the crustle base and bare baseline lineages to exploit the all-Grass Fire weakness.

layers.name: Mega Lucario (Legacy)
layers.description: Legacy Mega Lucario backup pilot retained as a historical baseline branch off main.py.

layers.name: Package & Tooling
layers.description: Package marker and tooling configuration that scaffold the agents package without participating in pilot strategy.

tour.title: The Production Pilot
tour.description: Start at main.py, the current production pilot and the root of the entire lineage. Every file in this folder follows the same contract it defines: a single agent(obs) function that takes the game observation and returns an action. main.py currently runs the strongest known strategy (Shun's Palace deck from the main_v3_pure lineage), so reading it first shows you both the shared scaffold and where the project's evolution has landed.
tour.languageLesson: Every pilot exposes a single top-level agent(obs) function as its public interface. The simulator imports this function by name, so the function signature is the contract — the rest of each file is private strategy logic supporting that one entry point.

tour.title: The Generic Auto-Role Engine
tour.description: bare_agent.py is the archetype-agnostic engine underneath everything. Instead of hardcoding which Pokemon attacks, it auto-assigns attacker and support roles from whatever cards are in play, making it deck-independent. This is the conceptual foundation: most later pilots are this generic engine plus small, deck-aware overlays. The fire.py counter deck even branches directly from it.

tour.title: Lineage Origins: v1
tour.description: main_v1.py is the first named iteration in the lineage chain that flows from main.py. It is also a hub: both the main line and the Honchkrow combo line (via main_honch_base.py) trace back through it, which is why it ranks high in fan-in. Reading it shows the earliest codified strategy that every later branch refines or reacts against.

tour.title: The v2 Branch Point
tour.description: main_v2.py is the busiest node in the graph by fan-out: it spawns five descendants (v3, v2_baseline, v2_rebel, the Typhlosion pilot, and feeds the v3-pure ablation). It introduces the Typhlosion-flavored generic engine. Critically, the untuned main_typhlosion.py from this line turned out to be the strongest base, teaching the recurring lesson that the generic engine beats over-tuning.

tour.title: The Crustle / Typhlosion Dual Line
tour.description: Building on the main line, main_crustle_base.py and main_typh_base.py form the dual-line deck branch. main_crustle_base.py is a high-fan-in hub feeding both the Typhlosion base and the fire counter deck, anchoring the two-Pokemon-line archetype. These pilots show how a base strategy gets specialized for a specific deck composition without abandoning the shared agent(obs) scaffold.

tour.title: The Key Ablation: v3 vs v3-pure
tour.description: This is the heart of the tour. main_v3.py is the full overlay pilot, layering many archetype heuristics on top of v2 and defining the _buddy_blast_damage helper. main_v3_pure.py keeps ONLY that one fix and discards every other overlay. The pure version wins 83.2% of the gauntlet while the full overlay regresses, proving the central project lesson: isolate the one correction the engine literally cannot read (real Buddy Blast damage), and never bundle speculative heuristics.
tour.languageLesson: _buddy_blast_damage encodes a game fact the generic engine cannot observe: an attack does 40 base, +60 more if Ethan's is in the discard pile. Both v3 and v3_pure call this same helper (note the shared call edge), so the only difference between them is what else got bundled around it — a clean ablation.

tour.title: The Hidden Win Condition: Honchkrow Combo
tour.description: honchkrow.py is a different philosophy: a combo pilot built around a hidden win condition rather than the generic engine. It derives from main_honch_base.py (which itself comes from v1) and centers on rocket_feathers_damage, the helper that computes the precise ammo-discard combo damage. Getting that discard accounting exactly right was the lever that pushed it to beat main.py around 56% of the time.

tour.title: The Hard Counter: Fire vs Grass
tour.description: fire.py demonstrates matchup-specific design rather than general strength. It branches from both main_crustle_base.py and the bare_agent.py generic engine, then specializes into Fire aggro that exploits main's all-Grass weakness, beating the production pilot 74% of the time. It is a reminder that strategy lives in the matchup: a deck need not be globally best to dominate one opponent.

tour.title: The Replaced Legacy: Mega Lucario
tour.description: Close with main_megalucario_backup.py, the legacy meta-aware multi-attacker pilot that the v3 line replaced. It branches directly from main.py and represents the prior generation of strategy. Reading it last lets you contrast the heavily meta-tuned ancestor against the lesson the lineage ultimately learned — that the lean generic engine plus a single surgical fix outperforms elaborate meta-awareness.
